Complete child blueprint graph 
{
  "meta": {
    "algorithm_name": "resource_film_dt",
    "description": "The algorithm augments the Decision Transformer with a **Resource‑Aware FiLM (Feature‑wise Linear Modulation) conditioning** layer.  \nA lightweight extractor pulls the two budget‑related dimensions (time_left, budget_left) from the state vector.  \nA FiLM module (implemented as a small MLP) maps this resource signal to per‑feature scaling (γ) and shifting (β) vectors, which modulate the Transformer’s hidden representation before the ActionHead.  \nBy conditioning the bid‑offset prediction on the current resource state, the policy can dynamically temper aggressiveness when budget or time is scarce, improving CPA compliance while retaining conversion performance.  \nAll existing components are retained; only the FiLM conditioning path and a new extractor are added, and the training stage includes the new learnable modules.",
    "parent": null,
    "version": "gen_1"
  },
  "design": {
    "core_insight": "Introduce Resource‑Aware FiLM conditioning that modulates transformer hidden states with budget and time signals, enabling the policy to adapt aggressiveness based on remaining resources and thereby improve CPA compliance.",
    "key_innovations": [
      {
        "name": "ResourceExtractor",
        "description": "A StateExtractor that pulls the first two state dimensions (time_left, budget_left).",
        "hypothesis": "Providing explicit resource signals lets the model learn when to back off bidding."
      },
      {
        "name": "FiLMConditioning",
        "description": "FiLM layer generates per‑feature scale (γ) and shift (β) from the resource vector via an MLP and applies γ·h + β to transformer outputs.",
        "hypothesis": "Feature‑wise modulation gives fine‑grained control, improving the trade‑off between conversions and CPA violations."
      }
    ],
    "new_operators": {
      "generated_lib.film.FiLM": {
        "description": "Feature‑wise Linear Modulation conditioned on a low‑dimensional resource vector.",
        "init_args": {
          "embed_dim": "int",
          "cond_dim": "int",
          "hidden_dim": "int"
        },
        "forward_inputs": {
          "features": "(B, T, embed_dim)",
          "resources": "(B, T, cond_dim)"
        },
        "forward_output": "(B, T, embed_dim)",
        "computation_logic": "MLP(resources) -> (B, T, 2*embed_dim); split -> gamma, beta; return gamma * features + beta"
      }
    }
  },
  "constants": {
    "embed_dim": 64,
    "transformer_layers": 3,
    "transformer_heads": 1,
    "mlp_dim": 512,
    "max_seq_len": 1024,
    "attn_dropout": 0.1,
    "resid_dropout": 0.1,
    "context_length": 20,
    "action_tanh": false,
    "state_dim": 16,
    "act_dim": 1,
    "max_ep_len": 96,
    "film_hidden_dim": 256
  },
  "graph": {
    "train": {
      "inputs": [
        "states",
        "actions",
        "rewards",
        "dones",
        "returns_to_go",
        "timesteps",
        "attention_mask",
        "expanded_attention_mask"
      ],
      "outputs": [
        "predicted_actions",
        "loss"
      ]
    },
    "nodes": {
      "state_embeddings": {
        "class_path": "lib.modules.dt.dt_encoder.StateEncoder",
        "init_args": {
          "state_dim": "!const state_dim",
          "embed_dim": "!const embed_dim",
          "max_ep_len": "!const max_ep_len"
        },
        "inputs": {
          "states": "states",
          "timesteps": "timesteps"
        },
        "expected_output": "(B, T, embed_dim)"
      },
      "action_embeddings": {
        "class_path": "lib.modules.dt.dt_encoder.ActionEncoder",
        "init_args": {
          "act_dim": "!const act_dim",
          "embed_dim": "!const embed_dim",
          "max_ep_len": "!const max_ep_len"
        },
        "inputs": {
          "actions": "actions",
          "timesteps": "timesteps"
        },
        "expected_output": "(B, T, embed_dim)"
      },
      "rtg_embeddings": {
        "class_path": "lib.modules.dt.dt_encoder.RTGEncoder",
        "init_args": {
          "embed_dim": "!const embed_dim",
          "max_ep_len": "!const max_ep_len"
        },
        "inputs": {
          "returns_to_go": "returns_to_go",
          "timesteps": "timesteps"
        },
        "expected_output": "(B, T, embed_dim)"
      },
      "interleaved_sequence": {
        "class_path": "lib.modules.dt.dt_interleave.InterleaveEmbeddings",
        "init_args": {
          "embed_dim": "!const embed_dim"
        },
        "inputs": {
          "state_embeddings": "state_embeddings",
          "action_embeddings": "action_embeddings",
          "rtg_embeddings": "rtg_embeddings"
        },
        "expected_output": "(B, 3*T, embed_dim)"
      },
      "transformer_output": {
        "class_path": "lib.modules.dt.dt_core.TransformerCore",
        "init_args": {
          "embed_dim": "!const embed_dim",
          "num_layers": "!const transformer_layers",
          "num_heads": "!const transformer_heads",
          "mlp_dim": "!const mlp_dim",
          "max_seq_len": "!const max_seq_len",
          "attn_dropout": "!const attn_dropout",
          "resid_dropout": "!const resid_dropout"
        },
        "inputs": {
          "sequence": "interleaved_sequence",
          "attention_mask": "attention_mask"
        },
        "expected_output": "(B, T, embed_dim)"
      },
      "resource_features": {
        "class_path": "lib.modules.ops.StateExtractor",
        "init_args": {
          "start_idx": 0,
          "end_idx": 2
        },
        "inputs": {
          "states": "states"
        },
        "expected_output": "(B, T, 2)"
      },
      "film_conditioning": {
        "class_path": "generated_lib.film.FiLM",
        "init_args": {
          "embed_dim": "!const embed_dim",
          "cond_dim": 2,
          "hidden_dim": "!const film_hidden_dim"
        },
        "inputs": {
          "features": "transformer_output",
          "resources": "resource_features"
        },
        "expected_output": "(B, T, embed_dim)"
      },
      "predicted_actions": {
        "class_path": "lib.modules.dt.dt_head.ActionHead",
        "init_args": {
          "embed_dim": "!const embed_dim",
          "act_dim": "!const act_dim",
          "action_tanh": "!const action_tanh"
        },
        "inputs": {
          "transformer_output": "film_conditioning"
        },
        "expected_output": "(B, T, act_dim)"
      },
      "loss": {
        "class_path": "lib.modules.losses.MaskedMSELoss",
        "init_args": {},
        "inputs": {
          "predictions": "predicted_actions",
          "targets": "actions",
          "mask": "attention_mask"
        },
        "expected_output": "scalar"
      }
    },
    "training_stages": [
      {
        "name": "main",
        "trainable_nodes": [
          "state_embeddings",
          "action_embeddings",
          "rtg_embeddings",
          "interleaved_sequence",
          "transformer_output",
          "film_conditioning",
          "predicted_actions"
        ],
        "loss_node": "loss",
        "checkpoint_prefix": "gave_dt_film"
      }
    ]
  }
}
Complete generated Python model 
"""Auto-generated standalone Python module from blueprint: resource_film_dt

This file is fully self-contained — no external dependencies beyond
PyTorch. All operator classes are inlined below.

The algorithm augments the Decision Transformer with a **Resource‑Aware FiLM (Feature‑wise Linear Modulation) conditioning** layer.
A lightweight extractor pulls the two budget‑related dimensions (t...

Generated from: blueprint.json
"""

import math

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from collections import OrderedDict
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple


# ======================================================================
# Library Operators (from graph_evolve/lib/modules/)
# ======================================================================

# --- Source: lib.modules.dt.dt_encoder.StateEncoder ---
class StateEncoder(nn.Module):
    """Encode raw state features into the transformer embedding space.

    Applies a linear projection followed by a learned timestep embedding.

    Args:
        state_dim: Dimension of raw state features (e.g., 16).
        embed_dim: Transformer hidden dimension (e.g., 64).
        max_ep_len: Maximum episode length for timestep embedding (e.g., 48).
    """

    def __init__(self, state_dim: int, embed_dim: int, max_ep_len: int = 48):
        super().__init__()
        self.embed_state = nn.Linear(state_dim, embed_dim)
        self.embed_timestep = nn.Embedding(max_ep_len, embed_dim)

    def forward(
        self, states: torch.Tensor, timesteps: torch.Tensor
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Encode states with timestep information.

        Args:
            states: Raw state features (B, T, state_dim).
            timesteps: Integer timestep indices (B, T).

        Returns:
            State embeddings (B, T, embed_dim).
        """
        return self.embed_state(states) + self.embed_timestep(timesteps)


class ActionEncoder(nn.Module):
    """Encode raw action values into the transformer embedding space.

    Applies a linear projection followed by a learned timestep embedding.

    Args:
        act_dim: Action dimension (e.g., 1).
        embed_dim: Transformer hidden dimension (e.g., 64).
        max_ep_len: Maximum episode length for timestep embedding (e.g., 48).
    """

    def __init__(self, act_dim: int, embed_dim: int, max_ep_len: int = 48):
        super().__init__()
        self.embed_action = nn.Linear(act_dim, embed_dim)
        self.embed_timestep = nn.Embedding(max_ep_len, embed_dim)

    def forward(
        self, actions: torch.Tensor, timesteps: torch.Tensor
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Encode actions with timestep information.

        Args:
            actions: Action values (B, T, act_dim).
            timesteps: Integer timestep indices (B, T).

        Returns:
            Action embeddings (B, T, embed_dim).
        """
        return self.embed_action(actions) + self.embed_timestep(timesteps)


class RTGEncoder(nn.Module):
    """Encode returns-to-go into the transformer embedding space.

    Applies a linear projection followed by a learned timestep embedding.

    Args:
        embed_dim: Transformer hidden dimension (e.g., 64).
        max_ep_len: Maximum episode length for timestep embedding (e.g., 48).
    """

    def __init__(self, embed_dim: int, max_ep_len: int = 48):
        super().__init__()
        self.embed_return = nn.Linear(1, embed_dim)
        self.embed_timestep = nn.Embedding(max_ep_len, embed_dim)

    def forward(
        self, returns_to_go: torch.Tensor, timesteps: torch.Tensor
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Encode returns-to-go with timestep information.

        Args:
            returns_to_go: RTG values (B, T, 1).
            timesteps: Integer timestep indices (B, T).

        Returns:
            RTG embeddings (B, T, embed_dim).
        """
        return self.embed_return(returns_to_go) + self.embed_timestep(timesteps)

# --- Source: lib.modules.ops.StateExtractor ---
class Identity(nn.Module):
    """Pass-through operator that returns its input unchanged.

    Useful as a no-op node in blueprint graphs, e.g. when the loss node
    needs to simply forward a single computed loss without modification.

    Accepts a single positional-or-keyword argument so that blueprints
    can wire it as ``inputs: {x: some_node}`` or ``inputs: {input: some_node}``.
    """

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return x


class Add(nn.Module):
    """Element-wise addition of two tensors (residual connection).

    The most common operation in blueprint graphs. Use this instead of
    creating a new AddTensors/ResidualAdd/SumTensor operator.
    """

    def forward(self, a: torch.Tensor, b: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return a + b


class WeightedAdd(nn.Module):
    """Learnable weighted addition: output = alpha * a + (1 - alpha) * b.

    A trainable residual connection where the model learns how much of
    each input to use. Recommended for balancing two loss terms: uses a
    single relative weight (sigmoid-constrained), which is more stable
    than two independent Scale nodes.
    """

    def __init__(self, init_alpha: float = 0.5):
        super().__init__()
        self.alpha = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(init_alpha))

    def forward(self, a: torch.Tensor, b: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        w = torch.sigmoid(self.alpha)
        return w * a + (1 - w) * b


class Concat(nn.Module):
    """Concatenate two tensors along the last dimension.

    Useful for combining different feature streams before a linear layer.
    """

    def forward(self, a: torch.Tensor, b: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return torch.cat([a, b], dim=-1)


class Scale(nn.Module):
    """Learnable scalar scaling: output = scale * input.

    Useful for controlling the magnitude of a residual branch or
    auxiliary signal before adding it to the main stream. Note: to
    balance two loss terms, prefer WeightedAdd over Scale+Add — it
    has one degree of freedom and avoids scale drift.
    """

    def __init__(self, init_scale: float = 1.0):
        super().__init__()
        self.scale = nn.Parameter(torch.tensor(init_scale))

    def forward(self, input: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.scale * input


class MLP(nn.Module):
    """Two-layer MLP with configurable activation and optional dropout.

    A general-purpose nonlinear transform. Can be used as a predictor
    head, feature transform, or value estimator.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        in_dim: int,
        hidden_dim: int,
        out_dim: int,
        dropout: float = 0.1,
        activation: str = "relu",
    ):
        super().__init__()
        act = {"relu": nn.ReLU, "gelu": nn.GELU, "tanh": nn.Tanh}
        act_cls = act.get(activation, nn.ReLU)
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(in_dim, hidden_dim),
            act_cls(),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, out_dim),
        )

    def forward(self, input: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.net(input)


class StateExtractor(nn.Module):
    """Extract specific feature indices from the state tensor.

    Useful for isolating budget/time features (indices 0-1) or market
    stats (indices 4-11) from the 16-dim state for specialized processing.
    """

    def __init__(self, start_idx: int, end_idx: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.start_idx = start_idx
        self.end_idx = end_idx

    def forward(self, states: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return states[..., self.start_idx : self.end_idx]

# --- Source: lib.modules.dt.dt_interleave.InterleaveEmbeddings ---
class InterleaveEmbeddings(nn.Module):
    """Interleave three embedding streams into (B, 3*T, embed_dim) — sequence length TRIPLES.

    Takes (RTG, State, Action) embeddings of shape (B, T, D) each,
    interleaves them into [RTG_1, S_1, A_1, RTG_2, S_2, A_2, ...],
    and applies LayerNorm.  The output length is 3*T, so ALL downstream
    nodes (attention_mask, timesteps, positional encoding) must also
    operate on length 3*T, not the original T.

    Returns a single tensor so that the LLM can freely insert
    additional operations (e.g., Add, Scale) between this node and
    TransformerCore without worrying about multi-output handling.

    Args:
        embed_dim: Embedding dimension (must match encoder outputs).
    """

    N_STREAMS = 3  # RTG, state, action

    def __init__(self, embed_dim: int = 64):
        super().__init__()
        self.embed_dim = embed_dim
        self.embed_ln = nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim)

    def forward(
        self,
        state_embeddings: torch.Tensor,
        action_embeddings: torch.Tensor,
        rtg_embeddings: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Interleave embedding streams into a single sequence.

        Args:
            state_embeddings: Encoded states (B, T, embed_dim).
            action_embeddings: Encoded actions (B, T, embed_dim).
            rtg_embeddings: Encoded returns-to-go (B, T, embed_dim).

        Returns:
            LayerNorm'd interleaved embeddings (B, 3*T, embed_dim).
        """
        batch_size, seq_length = state_embeddings.shape[:2]

        # Interleave: [RTG_1, S_1, A_1, RTG_2, S_2, A_2, ...]
        stacked = torch.stack(
            (rtg_embeddings, state_embeddings, action_embeddings), dim=1
        )  # (B, 3, T, D)
        stacked = stacked.permute(0, 2, 1, 3).reshape(
            batch_size, self.N_STREAMS * seq_length, self.embed_dim
        )
        return self.embed_ln(stacked)

# --- Source: lib.modules.dt.dt_core.TransformerCore ---
class _CausalSelfAttention(nn.Module):
    """Causal self-attention with pre-computed triangular mask."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        embed_dim: int,
        num_heads: int,
        max_seq_len: int,
        attn_dropout: float,
        resid_dropout: float,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        assert embed_dim % num_heads == 0
        self.key = nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim)
        self.query = nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim)
        self.value = nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim)
        self.attn_drop = nn.Dropout(attn_dropout)
        self.resid_drop = nn.Dropout(resid_dropout)
        self.register_buffer(
            "bias",
            torch.tril(torch.ones(max_seq_len, max_seq_len)).view(
                1, 1, max_seq_len, max_seq_len
            ),
        )
        self.register_buffer("masked_bias", torch.tensor(-1e4))
        self.proj = nn.Linear(embed_dim, embed_dim)
        self.n_head = num_heads

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        B, T, C = x.size()
        head_dim = C // self.n_head
        k = self.key(x).view(B, T, self.n_head, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
        q = self.query(x).view(B, T, self.n_head, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)
        v = self.value(x).view(B, T, self.n_head, head_dim).transpose(1, 2)

        mask_2d = mask.view(B, -1)[:, None, None, :]
        mask_bias = (1.0 - mask_2d) * -10000.0

        att = (q @ k.transpose(-2, -1)) * (1.0 / math.sqrt(k.size(-1)))
        att = torch.where(
            self.bias[:, :, :T, :T].bool(),
            att,
            self.masked_bias.to(att.dtype),
        )
        att = att + mask_bias
        att = F.softmax(att, dim=-1)
        att = self.attn_drop(att)
        y = (att @ v).transpose(1, 2).contiguous().view(B, T, C)
        return self.resid_drop(self.proj(y))


class _Block(nn.Module):
    """Transformer block: LayerNorm → Attention → LayerNorm → MLP."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        embed_dim: int,
        num_heads: int,
        mlp_dim: int,
        max_seq_len: int,
        attn_dropout: float,
        resid_dropout: float,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.ln1 = nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim)
        self.ln2 = nn.LayerNorm(embed_dim)
        self.attn = _CausalSelfAttention(
            embed_dim, num_heads, max_seq_len, attn_dropout, resid_dropout
        )
        self.mlp = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(embed_dim, mlp_dim),
            nn.GELU(),
            nn.Linear(mlp_dim, embed_dim),
            nn.Dropout(resid_dropout),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, mask: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = x + self.attn(self.ln1(x), mask)
        x = x + self.mlp(self.ln2(x))
        return x


class TransformerCore(nn.Module):
    """Causal Transformer that processes a pre-interleaved sequence.

    Receives a single interleaved causal sequence of shape
    (B, 3*T, embed_dim) — already arranged as
    [RTG_1, S_1, A_1, RTG_2, S_2, A_2, ...] by an upstream
    InterleaveEmbeddings operator — and processes it through a stack
    of Transformer blocks.

    After processing, extracts the state-position hidden representations
    (index 1 in each [RTG, S, A] triplet) and returns them.

    The output can be routed to any downstream head (action prediction,
    value estimation, etc.).

    Args:
        embed_dim: Transformer hidden dimension (e.g., 64).
        num_layers: Number of Transformer blocks (e.g., 3).
        num_heads: Number of attention heads (e.g., 1).
        mlp_dim: MLP hidden dimension inside each block (e.g., 512).
        max_seq_len: Maximum interleaved sequence length for causal mask (e.g., 1024).
        attn_dropout: Attention dropout probability.
        resid_dropout: Residual/MLP dropout probability.
        num_streams: Number of interleaved streams (default 3: RTG, state, action).
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        embed_dim: int = 64,
        num_layers: int = 3,
        num_heads: int = 1,
        mlp_dim: int = 512,
        max_seq_len: int = 1024,
        attn_dropout: float = 0.1,
        resid_dropout: float = 0.1,
        num_streams: int = 3,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.embed_dim = embed_dim
        self.num_streams = num_streams
        self.transformer = nn.ModuleList(
            [
                _Block(
                    embed_dim,
                    num_heads,
                    mlp_dim,
                    max_seq_len,
                    attn_dropout,
                    resid_dropout,
                )
                for _ in range(num_layers)
            ]
        )

    def forward(
        self,
        sequence: torch.Tensor,
        attention_mask: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Process a pre-interleaved sequence through the Transformer.

        Args:
            sequence: Interleaved embeddings (B, num_streams*T, embed_dim),
                already LayerNorm'd by the upstream InterleaveEmbeddings.
            attention_mask: Original validity mask (B, T), 1=valid, 0=padding.
                Automatically expanded to (B, num_streams*T) internally.

        Returns:
            State-position hidden representations (B, T, embed_dim).
        """
        batch_size = sequence.shape[0]
        total_length = sequence.shape[1]

        # When num_streams == 1 (non-interleaved input), skip the
        # stream-expansion and state-extraction logic entirely.
        # This allows blueprints that concatenate/project embeddings
        # into a single (B, T, embed_dim) stream to use TransformerCore
        # directly without InterleaveEmbeddings.
        if self.num_streams == 1:
            mask = attention_mask.to(sequence.dtype)
            # If mask length doesn't match sequence length, try to
            # use it as-is (custom upstream may have pre-expanded it).
            if mask.shape[1] != total_length:
                # Truncate or pad to match — but in practice this
                # shouldn't happen for well-formed blueprints.
                mask = mask[:, :total_length] if mask.shape[1] > total_length else mask
            hidden = sequence
            for block in self.transformer:
                hidden = block(hidden, mask)
            return hidden  # (B, T, embed_dim)

        seq_length = total_length // self.num_streams

        # Validate that total_length is divisible by num_streams
        if total_length % self.num_streams != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"TransformerCore: sequence length {total_length} is not "
                f"divisible by num_streams={self.num_streams}. "
                f"If you are NOT using InterleaveEmbeddings, set "
                f"num_streams=1 in TransformerCore's init_args. "
                f"If you ARE using InterleaveEmbeddings with "
                f"{self.num_streams} streams, ensure the upstream "
                f"sequence has length {self.num_streams}*T."
            )

        # Expand mask from (B, T) to (B, num_streams*T) to match interleaved layout
        if attention_mask.shape[1] == seq_length:
            expanded_mask = (
                attention_mask.unsqueeze(1)
                .expand(-1, self.num_streams, -1)  # (B, num_streams, T)
                .permute(0, 2, 1)                  # (B, T, num_streams)
                .reshape(batch_size, total_length)
                .to(sequence.dtype)
            )
        else:
            # Mask is already expanded (e.g., by a custom upstream node)
            expanded_mask = attention_mask.to(sequence.dtype)

        # Process through Transformer blocks
        hidden = sequence
        for block in self.transformer:
            hidden = block(hidden, expanded_mask)

        # Extract state-position outputs (index 1 in each [RTG, S, A] triplet)
        hidden = hidden.reshape(
            batch_size, seq_length, self.num_streams, self.embed_dim
        )
        return hidden[:, :, 1, :]  # (B, T, embed_dim)

# --- Source: lib.modules.dt.dt_head.ActionHead ---
class ActionHead(nn.Module):
    """Predict actions from transformer hidden representations.

    A single linear layer mapping from embed_dim to act_dim, with
    optional tanh activation for bounded action spaces.

    Args:
        embed_dim: Transformer hidden dimension (e.g., 64).
        act_dim: Action dimension (e.g., 1).
        action_tanh: Whether to apply tanh to bound outputs.
    """

    def __init__(
        self, embed_dim: int = 64, act_dim: int = 1, action_tanh: bool = False
    ):
        super().__init__()
        layers = [nn.Linear(embed_dim, act_dim)]
        if action_tanh:
            layers.append(nn.Tanh())
        self.predict_action = nn.Sequential(*layers)

    def forward(self, transformer_output: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Predict actions from transformer state-position outputs.

        Args:
            transformer_output: Hidden representations (B, T, embed_dim).

        Returns:
            Predicted actions (B, T, act_dim).
        """
        return self.predict_action(transformer_output)

# --- Source: lib.modules.losses.MaskedMSELoss ---
class MaskedMSELoss(nn.Module):
    """Compute MSE loss over valid (masked) positions only.

    Flattens predictions and targets along the batch and time dimensions,
    selects only positions where mask > 0, and returns the mean squared
    error over those positions.

    Args:
        None — this module has no learnable parameters.
    """

    def forward(
        self,
        predictions: torch.Tensor,
        targets: torch.Tensor,
        mask: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Compute masked MSE loss.

        Args:
            predictions: Predicted values (B, T, D).
            targets: Ground-truth values (B, T, D).
            mask: Validity mask (B, T), 1 = valid, 0 = padding.

        Returns:
            Scalar MSE loss over valid positions.
        """
        # Shape diagnostic — purpose-limited: only catch the case where
        # the existing implementation would otherwise raise a *cryptic*
        # PyTorch boolean-indexing error ("shape of the mask [N] does
        # not match the indexed tensor [M, D]").  In that exact case,
        # surface a framework-level error message that names the contract
        # and the observed shapes so the fix-loop can act on it directly.
        #
        # We deliberately do NOT validate predictions.shape == targets.shape:
        # the original implementation accepts any pair whose reshape to
        # (-1, D) is mutually broadcast-compatible, and rejecting more
        # patterns than that would shrink the design space the LLM is
        # free to explore.
        feature_dim = predictions.shape[-1]
        leading = predictions.numel() // feature_dim if feature_dim > 0 else 0
        if leading != mask.numel():
            raise ValueError(
                f"MaskedMSELoss shape contract: predictions=(B, T, D), "
                f"mask=(B, T). The product of leading dims of predictions "
                f"must equal mask.numel(). Got "
                f"predictions={tuple(predictions.shape)} "
                f"(leading={leading}, D={feature_dim}), "
                f"mask={tuple(mask.shape)} (numel={mask.numel()}). "
                f"Common cause: the upstream node reduced or aggregated "
                f"the (B, T) dimension before reaching this loss."
            )
        predictions_flat = predictions.reshape(-1, feature_dim)[mask.reshape(-1) > 0]
        targets_flat = targets.reshape(-1, feature_dim)[mask.reshape(-1) > 0]
        return torch.mean((predictions_flat - targets_flat) ** 2)


# ======================================================================
# Generated Operators (from generated_lib/)
# ======================================================================

# --- Source: generated_lib.film.FiLM ---
class FiLM(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, embed_dim: int, cond_dim: int, hidden_dim: int):
        super().__init__()
        self.mlp = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(cond_dim, hidden_dim),
            nn.ReLU(),
            nn.Linear(hidden_dim, 2 * embed_dim)
        )

    def forward(self, features: torch.Tensor, resources: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # resources: (B, T, cond_dim)
        # features:  (B, T, embed_dim)
        params = self.mlp(resources)  # (B, T, 2*embed_dim)
        gamma, beta = torch.chunk(params, 2, dim=-1)  # each (B, T, embed_dim)
        return gamma * features + beta


# ======================================================================
# Constants
# ======================================================================

CONSTANTS = {
    'embed_dim': 64,
    'transformer_layers': 3,
    'transformer_heads': 1,
    'mlp_dim': 512,
    'max_seq_len': 1024,
    'attn_dropout': 0.1,
    'resid_dropout': 0.1,
    'context_length': 20,
    'action_tanh': False,
    'state_dim': 16,
    'act_dim': 1,
    'max_ep_len': 96,
    'film_hidden_dim': 256,
}


# ======================================================================
# BlueprintModel
# ======================================================================

class BlueprintModel(nn.Module):
    """Blueprint-assembled model: resource_film_dt

    Train inputs: ['states', 'actions', 'rewards', 'dones', 'returns_to_go', 'timesteps', 'attention_mask', 'expanded_attention_mask']
    Train outputs: ['predicted_actions', 'loss']
    Nodes: 9
    Execution order: ['state_embeddings', 'action_embeddings', 'rtg_embeddings', 'resource_features', 'interleaved_sequence', 'transformer_output', 'film_conditioning', 'predicted_actions', 'loss']
    """

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()

        self.state_embeddings = StateEncoder(state_dim=16, embed_dim=64, max_ep_len=96)
        self.action_embeddings = ActionEncoder(act_dim=1, embed_dim=64, max_ep_len=96)
        self.rtg_embeddings = RTGEncoder(embed_dim=64, max_ep_len=96)
        self.interleaved_sequence = InterleaveEmbeddings(embed_dim=64)
        self.transformer_output = TransformerCore(embed_dim=64, num_layers=3, num_heads=1, mlp_dim=512, max_seq_len=1024, attn_dropout=0.1, resid_dropout=0.1)
        self.resource_features = StateExtractor(start_idx=0, end_idx=2)
        self.film_conditioning = FiLM(embed_dim=64, cond_dim=2, hidden_dim=256)
        self.predicted_actions = ActionHead(embed_dim=64, act_dim=1, action_tanh=False)
        self.loss = MaskedMSELoss()

    def forward(
        self,
        states: torch.Tensor,
        actions: torch.Tensor,
        rewards: torch.Tensor,
        dones: torch.Tensor,
        returns_to_go: torch.Tensor,
        timesteps: torch.Tensor,
        attention_mask: torch.Tensor,
        expanded_attention_mask: torch.Tensor,
    ) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
        """Execute the computation graph."""

        # Data dict holds all intermediate results
        data = {
            'states': states,
            'actions': actions,
            'rewards': rewards,
            'dones': dones,
            'returns_to_go': returns_to_go,
            'timesteps': timesteps,
            'attention_mask': attention_mask,
            'expanded_attention_mask': expanded_attention_mask,
        }

        # Node: state_embeddings
        #   class: lib.modules.dt.dt_encoder.StateEncoder
        data['state_embeddings'] = self.state_embeddings(states=data['states'], timesteps=data['timesteps'])

        # Node: action_embeddings
        #   class: lib.modules.dt.dt_encoder.ActionEncoder
        data['action_embeddings'] = self.action_embeddings(actions=data['actions'], timesteps=data['timesteps'])

        # Node: rtg_embeddings
        #   class: lib.modules.dt.dt_encoder.RTGEncoder
        data['rtg_embeddings'] = self.rtg_embeddings(returns_to_go=data['returns_to_go'], timesteps=data['timesteps'])

        # Node: resource_features
        #   class: lib.modules.ops.StateExtractor
        data['resource_features'] = self.resource_features(states=data['states'])

        # Node: interleaved_sequence
        #   class: lib.modules.dt.dt_interleave.InterleaveEmbeddings
        data['interleaved_sequence'] = self.interleaved_sequence(state_embeddings=data['state_embeddings'], action_embeddings=data['action_embeddings'], rtg_embeddings=data['rtg_embeddings'])

        # Node: transformer_output
        #   class: lib.modules.dt.dt_core.TransformerCore
        data['transformer_output'] = self.transformer_output(sequence=data['interleaved_sequence'], attention_mask=data['attention_mask'])

        # Node: film_conditioning
        #   class: generated_lib.film.FiLM
        data['film_conditioning'] = self.film_conditioning(features=data['transformer_output'], resources=data['resource_features'])

        # Node: predicted_actions
        #   class: lib.modules.dt.dt_head.ActionHead
        data['predicted_actions'] = self.predicted_actions(transformer_output=data['film_conditioning'])

        # Node: loss
        #   class: lib.modules.losses.MaskedMSELoss
        data['loss'] = self.loss(predictions=data['predicted_actions'], targets=data['actions'], mask=data['attention_mask'])

        return {
            'predicted_actions': data['predicted_actions'],
            'loss': data['loss'],
        }


# ======================================================================
# Training Stages
# ======================================================================

TRAINING_STAGES = [
    {
        'name': 'main',
        'loss_node': 'loss',
        'trainable_nodes': ['state_embeddings', 'action_embeddings', 'rtg_embeddings', 'interleaved_sequence', 'transformer_output', 'film_conditioning', 'predicted_actions'],
        'checkpoint_prefix': 'gave_dt_film',
    },
]


# ======================================================================
# Standalone Training & Evaluation
# ======================================================================


import ast
import os
import sys
import json
import random
import pickle
import argparse
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset, WeightedRandomSampler
from tqdm import tqdm

try:
    import scipy.special
except ImportError:
    scipy = None

try:
    from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
except ImportError:
    LinearRegression = None


def set_global_seed(seed: int):
    np.random.seed(seed)
    random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed)
    torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(seed)


class EpisodeReplayBuffer(Dataset):
    """Replay buffer that loads trajectory data from CSV."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        state_dim: int,
        act_dim: int,
        data_path: str,
        max_ep_len: int = 48,
        scale: float = 100.0,
        K: int = 20,
        weighting_standard: str = "RW",
        alpha: float = 0.1,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.max_ep_len = max_ep_len
        self.scale = scale
        self.state_dim = state_dim
        self.act_dim = act_dim
        self.alpha = alpha
        self.K = K

        def safe_literal_eval(val):
            if pd.isna(val):
                return val
            try:
                return ast.literal_eval(val)
            except (ValueError, SyntaxError):
                return val

        training_data = pd.read_csv(data_path)
        training_data["state"] = training_data["state"].apply(safe_literal_eval)
        training_data["next_state"] = training_data["next_state"].apply(safe_literal_eval)

        self.states, self.rewards, self.actions = [], [], []
        self.returns, self.traj_lens, self.dones = [], [], []
        state, reward, action, dones = [], [], [], []

        for _, row in training_data.iterrows():
            state.append(row["state"])
            reward.append(row["reward"])
            action.append(row["action"])
            dones.append(row["done"])
            if row["done"]:
                if len(state) != 1:
                    self.states.append(np.array(state))
                    self.rewards.append(np.expand_dims(np.array(reward), axis=1))
                    self.actions.append(np.expand_dims(np.array(action), axis=1))
                    self.returns.append(sum(reward))
                    self.traj_lens.append(len(state))
                    self.dones.append(np.array(dones))
                state, reward, action, dones = [], [], [], []

        self.traj_lens = np.array(self.traj_lens)
        self.returns = np.array(self.returns)

        tmp_states = np.concatenate(self.states, axis=0)
        self.state_mean = np.mean(tmp_states, axis=0)
        self.state_std = np.std(tmp_states, axis=0) + 1e-6

        self.trajectories = []
        for i in range(len(self.states)):
            self.trajectories.append({
                "observations": self.states[i],
                "actions": self.actions[i],
                "rewards": self.rewards[i],
                "dones": self.dones[i],
            })

        self.pct_traj = 1.0
        num_timesteps = max(int(self.pct_traj * sum(self.traj_lens)), 1)
        sorted_inds = np.argsort(self.returns)
        num_trajectories = 1
        timesteps = self.traj_lens[sorted_inds[-1]]
        ind = len(self.trajectories) - 2
        while ind >= 0 and timesteps + self.traj_lens[sorted_inds[ind]] <= num_timesteps:
            timesteps += self.traj_lens[sorted_inds[ind]]
            num_trajectories += 1
            ind -= 1
        self.sorted_inds = sorted_inds[-num_trajectories:]

        self.normalized_returns = (
            (self.returns[self.sorted_inds] - self.returns.min())
            / (self.returns.ptp() + 1e-8)
        )

        if weighting_standard == "RW" and scipy is not None:
            self.p_sample = scipy.special.softmax(self.normalized_returns / self.alpha)
        elif weighting_standard == "AW" and scipy is not None and LinearRegression is not None:
            s0 = np.stack(
                [e["observations"][0] for e in self.trajectories]
            )[self.sorted_inds]
            V = LinearRegression().fit(s0, self.normalized_returns).predict(s0)
            A = self.normalized_returns - V
            self.p_sample = scipy.special.softmax(A / self.alpha)
        elif weighting_standard == "LW":
            self.p_sample = (
                self.traj_lens[self.sorted_inds]
                / sum(self.traj_lens[self.sorted_inds])
            )
        else:
            self.p_sample = np.ones(len(self.sorted_inds)) / len(self.sorted_inds)

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.sorted_inds)

    def __getitem__(self, index):
        traj = self.trajectories[int(self.sorted_inds[index])]
        max_start_t = traj["rewards"].shape[0] - self.K
        start_t = random.randint(0, max(max_start_t, 0))

        s = traj["observations"][start_t : start_t + self.K]
        a = traj["actions"][start_t : start_t + self.K]
        r = traj["rewards"][start_t : start_t + self.K].reshape(-1, 1)
        d = traj.get("terminals", traj["dones"])[start_t : start_t + self.K]
        timesteps = np.arange(start_t, start_t + s.shape[0])
        timesteps = np.clip(timesteps, 0, self.max_ep_len - 1)

        rtg = self._discount_cumsum(traj["rewards"][start_t:], gamma=1.0)[
            : s.shape[0] + 1
        ].reshape(-1, 1)
        if rtg.shape[0] <= s.shape[0]:
            rtg = np.concatenate([rtg, np.zeros((1, 1))], axis=0)

        tlen = s.shape[0]
        s = np.concatenate([np.zeros((self.K - tlen, self.state_dim)), s], axis=0)
        s = (s - self.state_mean) / self.state_std
        a = np.concatenate(
            [np.ones((self.K - tlen, self.act_dim)) * -10.0, a], axis=0
        ) / self.scale
        r = np.concatenate([np.zeros((self.K - tlen, 1)), r], axis=0) / self.scale
        d = np.concatenate([np.ones((self.K - tlen)) * 2, d], axis=0)
        rtg = np.concatenate(
            [np.zeros((self.K - tlen, 1)), rtg], axis=0
        ) / self.scale
        timesteps = np.concatenate([np.zeros((self.K - tlen)), timesteps], axis=0)
        mask = np.concatenate([np.zeros((self.K - tlen)), np.ones(tlen)], axis=0)

        return (
            torch.from_numpy(s).float(),
            torch.from_numpy(a).float(),
            torch.from_numpy(r).float(),
            torch.from_numpy(d).long(),
            torch.from_numpy(rtg).float(),
            torch.from_numpy(timesteps).long(),
            torch.from_numpy(mask),
        )

    def _discount_cumsum(self, x, gamma=1.0):
        out = np.zeros_like(x)
        out[-1] = x[-1]
        for t in reversed(range(x.shape[0] - 1)):
            out[t] = x[t] + gamma * out[t + 1]
        return out


def train(
    data_path: str,
    save_dir: str = "results",
    num_steps: int = 2000,
    batch_size: int = 64,
    lr: float = 0.0001,
    weight_decay: float = 0.0001,
    grad_clip: float = 0.25,
    warmup_steps: int = 10000,
    seed: int = 1,
    device: str = "cuda",
    weighting: str = "RW",
    alpha: float = 0.1,
):
    """Train the BlueprintModel on offline trajectory data.

    Args:
        data_path: Path to training CSV file.
        save_dir: Directory to save model checkpoints.
        num_steps: Number of training iterations.
        batch_size: Training batch size.
        lr: Learning rate.
        weight_decay: AdamW weight decay.
        grad_clip: Gradient clipping norm.
        warmup_steps: Linear warmup steps for LR scheduler.
        seed: Random seed.
        device: 'cuda' or 'cpu'.
        weighting: Sampling strategy ('RW', 'AW', 'LW', 'uniform').
        alpha: Temperature for weighted sampling.

    Returns:
        Tuple of (model, save_path, training_stats).
    """
    set_global_seed(seed)
    os.makedirs(save_dir, exist_ok=True)

    state_dim = CONSTANTS.get("state_dim", 16)
    act_dim = CONSTANTS.get("act_dim", 1)
    context_length = CONSTANTS.get("context_length", 20)
    max_ep_len = CONSTANTS.get("max_ep_len", 48)
    scale = CONSTANTS.get("scale", 100)

    # Build replay buffer
    replay_buffer = EpisodeReplayBuffer(
        state_dim=state_dim,
        act_dim=act_dim,
        data_path=data_path,
        max_ep_len=max_ep_len,
        scale=scale,
        K=context_length,
        weighting_standard=weighting,
        alpha=alpha,
    )
    print(f"[INFO] Loaded {len(replay_buffer.trajectories)} trajectories from {data_path}")

    # Build model
    model = BlueprintModel().to(device)
    total_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters())
    trainable_params = sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad)
    print(f"[INFO] Model parameters: {total_params:,} total, {trainable_params:,} trainable")

    # Optimizer + scheduler
    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=lr, weight_decay=weight_decay)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(
        optimizer, lambda steps: min((steps + 1) / warmup_steps, 1)
    )

    # DataLoader with weighted sampling
    sampler = WeightedRandomSampler(
        replay_buffer.p_sample,
        num_samples=num_steps * batch_size,
        replacement=True,
    )
    dataloader = DataLoader(replay_buffer, sampler=sampler, batch_size=batch_size)

    # Save normalization stats
    with open(os.path.join(save_dir, "normalize_dict.pkl"), "wb") as f:
        pickle.dump({
            "state_mean": replay_buffer.state_mean,
            "state_std": replay_buffer.state_std,
        }, f)

    config = {
        "state_dim": state_dim,
        "act_dim": act_dim,
        "context_length": context_length,
        "max_ep_len": max_ep_len,
        "scale": scale,
        "seed": seed,
        "num_steps": num_steps,
        "batch_size": batch_size,
        "lr": lr,
        "state_mean": replay_buffer.state_mean.tolist(),
        "state_std": replay_buffer.state_std.tolist(),
    }
    with open(os.path.join(save_dir, "param.json"), "w") as f:
        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)

    # Training loop
    model.train()
    losses = []
    pbar = tqdm(enumerate(dataloader), total=num_steps, desc="Training", unit="step")

    for i, (states, actions, rewards, dones, rtg, timesteps, attention_mask) in pbar:
        states = states.to(device)
        actions = actions.to(device)
        rewards = rewards.to(device)
        dones = dones.to(device)
        rtg = rtg.to(device)
        timesteps = timesteps.to(device)
        attention_mask = attention_mask.to(device)

        outputs = model(
            states=states,
            actions=actions,
            rewards=rewards,
            dones=dones,
            returns_to_go=rtg[:, :-1],
            timesteps=timesteps,
            attention_mask=attention_mask,
        )

        loss = outputs["balanced_total_loss"]
        if loss.numel() != 1:
            loss = loss.mean()

        optimizer.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()
        torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), grad_clip)
        optimizer.step()
        scheduler.step()

        loss_val = loss.detach().cpu().item()
        losses.append(loss_val)
        pbar.set_postfix(loss=f"{loss_val:.6f}", lr=f"{optimizer.param_groups[0]['lr']:.2e}")

        if (i + 1) % 200 == 0:
            print(f"[INFO] Step {i + 1}/{num_steps}  Loss: {loss_val:.6f}")

    # Save final model
    save_path = os.path.join(save_dir, f"model_seed_{seed}_step_{num_steps}.pt")
    torch.save(model.state_dict(), save_path)
    print(f"[INFO] Training complete. Model saved to {save_path}")

    training_stats = {
        "loss_start": losses[0] if losses else 0,
        "loss_end": losses[-1] if losses else 0,
        "loss_min": min(losses) if losses else 0,
    }
    with open(os.path.join(save_dir, "training_stats.json"), "w") as f:
        json.dump(training_stats, f, indent=2)

    return model, save_path, training_stats


if __name__ == "__main__":
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Train and evaluate the assembled blueprint model."
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--data_path", type=str, required=True,
        help="Path to training data CSV file",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--save_dir", type=str, default="results",
        help="Directory to save model checkpoints and results",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--num_steps", type=int, default=2000,
        help="Number of training steps",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--batch_size", type=int, default=64,
        help="Training batch size",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--lr", type=float, default=0.0001,
        help="Learning rate",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--weight_decay", type=float, default=0.0001,
        help="AdamW weight decay",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--grad_clip", type=float, default=0.25,
        help="Gradient clipping norm",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--warmup_steps", type=int, default=10000,
        help="LR warmup steps",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--seed", type=int, default=1,
        help="Random seed",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--device", type=str, default="cuda",
        help="Device: 'cuda' or 'cpu'",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--weighting", type=str, default="RW",
        choices=["RW", "AW", "LW", "uniform"],
        help="Sampling strategy for replay buffer",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--alpha", type=float, default=0.1,
        help="Temperature for weighted sampling",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    model, save_path, stats = train(
        data_path=args.data_path,
        save_dir=args.save_dir,
        num_steps=args.num_steps,
        batch_size=args.batch_size,
        lr=args.lr,
        weight_decay=args.weight_decay,
        grad_clip=args.grad_clip,
        warmup_steps=args.warmup_steps,
        seed=args.seed,
        device=args.device,
        weighting=args.weighting,
        alpha=args.alpha,
    )

    print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")
    print(f"  Training Results")
    print(f"{'=' * 60}")
    print(f"  Loss start: {stats['loss_start']:.6f}")
    print(f"  Loss end:   {stats['loss_end']:.6f}")
    print(f"  Loss min:   {stats['loss_min']:.6f}")
    print(f"  Model:      {save_path}")
    print(f"{'=' * 60}")
693.8120Gen 1 evaluation score
+9.5599Change from Gen 0
No errorCandidate entered the population
04

Wiki knowledge base maintenance

ADAGE maintains the Wiki knowledge base by recording each evaluated generation, compiling the record into linked Wiki pages, and periodically rewriting the overview document. The first three modules trace this process, and the final section visualizes the resulting Wiki page graph.

04.1

Generation recording

The complete evaluation outcome is stored as an immutable generation record.

Generation ID1
Parent ID0
Child blueprintresource_film_dt snapshot
Evaluation score693.8120
Task metricsScore 336.0828 · Conversions 357.7292
Execution errorNone
04.2

Wiki page compilation

An LLM compiles the generation record and the existing page index into a typed page, then adds semantic links to related Wiki pages.

Wiki compiler prompt · Generation 1

SYSTEM
Compile the evaluated outcome into a linked Wiki page.
Extract a reusable finding rather than an experiment narrative.

INPUT · GENERATION RECORD
Generation: 1
Parent: 0
Child blueprint: resource_film_dt
Evaluation score: 693.8120
Task metrics:
  Score: 336.0828
  Conversions: 357.7292
Execution error: none

INPUT · WIKI PAGE INDEX
Page titles and page types already stored in the Wiki page graph.
Use the index to connect this finding to related knowledge.

INPUT · COMPLETED LITERATURE REPORTS
None available for this update.

RETURN
<KNOWLEDGE_TYPE> page type </KNOWLEDGE_TYPE>
<PAGE_NAME> snake_case page name </PAGE_NAME>
<ANALYSIS> reusable finding, scope, and Wiki links </ANALYSIS>
<OPERATOR_INSIGHT> operator finding or none </OPERATOR_INSIGHT>
<OVERVIEW_UPDATE> concise update for later overview rewriting </OVERVIEW_UPDATE>

Compiled Wiki page · Actual output

<KNOWLEDGE_TYPE>
effective_pattern
</KNOWLEDGE_TYPE>

<PAGE_NAME>
resource_aware_film_conditioning
</PAGE_NAME>

<ANALYSIS>
+9.6 evaluation score (+1.4% over the parent) after
conditioning transformer hidden states on remaining budget
and time through a lightweight FiLM layer. Multiplicative
scaling and additive shifting adjust the policy representation
before the ActionHead.

Related Wiki pages:
[[budget_conditioning_embedding]]
[[fiLM_scaling_gating_rule]]
</ANALYSIS>

<OPERATOR_INSIGHT>
resource_film_shape_alignment
FiLM inputs must retain the canonical (B,T,D) interface;
resource features are projected before feature-wise modulation.
</OPERATOR_INSIGHT>

The page becomes a node in the Wiki page graph. Its Wiki links create semantic edges to related pages. Completed literature reports follow the same compilation path asynchronously and produce literature analysis pages.

04.3

Overview document update

Every five generations in this run, the updater rewrites the overview document from the previous overview and the newly cached Wiki pages.

Previous overview documentCached Wiki pagesCompleted generations
Gen 5 trajectory summary

Gen 1 · 693.8 · +9.6: resource-aware FiLM establishes the initial improvement. The updated overview then relates this result to the MoE, hierarchical FiLM, scheduler, and temperature variants generated in Gen 2–5.

Complete overview document after Generation 5 
# Evolution Overview

> This overview distills cross‑run knowledge from the latest sequence‑decision evolutions. It is **purely descriptive** except for the **Promising Directions** section, which proposes forward‑looking experiments.

---

## 1. Trajectory Summary

| Gen | Score | Δ vs Parent | Core Change | Key Outcome |
|-----|-------|-------------|-------------|-------------|
| 1 | 693.8 | +9.6 | Introduced resource‑aware FiLM conditioning (budget + time tokens) | Baseline FiLM improves CPA compliance. |
| 2 | 689.1 | –4.7 | Added resource‑conditioned MoE + load‑balancing aux loss (≤5 % budget) | Slight decay; routing ineffective, auxiliary too small. |
| 3 | 528.8 | –165.0 | Stacked global + local FiLM **and** applied budget regularizer *without* detachment/cap | Severe gradient conflict; collapse. |
| 4 | 694.4 | +5.3 | Replaced MoE by a shallow **Resource‑Token Scheduler** (MLP on raw budget‑time) feeding FiLM; removed routing loss | Simpler token flow restores gradient magnitude → net gain. |
| 5 | 699.9 | +5.5 | Added **timestep‑driven temperature** (bounded MLP) that anneals FiLM strength | Early‑episode aggressiveness, late‑episode conservatism → highest score. |

*Earlier*: The first generation built the FiLM foundation; subsequent attempts explored MoE, hierarchical FiLM, and finally converged on a scheduler + temperature scheme.

---

## 2. Effective Patterns

| # | Core Mechanism (merged) | Why It Helps (theory) | When It May Fail (limits) |
|---|--------------------------|------------------------|---------------------------|
| 1️⃣ | **Resource‑aware FiLM with gated scaling** – multiplicative gain (≈0.1 – 0.2) on a resource token, optionally modulated by a per‑step temperature MLP (bounded sigmoid/tanh). | Keeps the base representation direction while aligning modulation magnitude with remaining budget; temperature annealing enforces a monotonic aggressiveness schedule without extra losses (linear‑approximation and policy‑gradient variance reduction). | – Saturating gain (> 2) kills gradients. – Unbounded temperature amplifies FiLM gradients → instability. |
| 2️⃣ | **Hierarchical FiLM (global → per‑step) with residual shortcut** – global FiLM feeds a residual path; per‑step FiLM inserted **before** the residual addition. | Global conditioning supplies long‑range constraints; per‑step FiLM refines decisions; residual ensures gradient flow even if the second FiLM is weak (pre‑activation principle). | – Competing affine transforms create an ill‑conditioned Jacobian unless the per‑step scale is initialised near 0 and capped. |
| 3️⃣ | **Capped, detached auxiliary losses** (budget regularizer, safety critic, dynamics predictor) limited to ≤ 15 % of total gradient norm via an EMA‑based balancer. | Guarantees auxiliary bias without overwhelming the primary MSE; detachment prevents unwanted gradient coupling (adaptive regularisation theory). | – Omitting the cap or detaching leads to loss domination and collapse. |
| 4️⃣ | **Modality‑specific expert routing (MoE) conditioned on the same resource token** – routing logits receive the FiLM token; routing loss respects the global auxiliary budget (≥10 %). | Sparse experts specialise per modality; resource conditioning aligns expert choice with constraints, improving compute efficiency (conditional computation theory). | – Under‑budgeted routing loss (< 5 %) yields uniform routing and gradient dilution; shape mismatches break the pipeline. |
| 5️⃣ | **Token‑level resource embedding + bounded hybrid positional encoding** – dense per‑step vector + sinusoidal + learned + stochastic components projected then tanh‑bounded. | Turns the problem into a partially observable MDP with explicit state, reducing policy‑gradient variance; bounded codes keep optimisation well‑conditioned. | – Removing the embedding or using unbounded encodings re‑introduces scale drift. |
| 6️⃣ | **Residual‑before‑modulation for dilated‑neighbourhood attention or causal dilated convolutions** – FiLM applied before the residual addition. | Preserves gradient flow through both the dilated core and the original token stream (pre‑activation). | – Over‑aggressive clamping of FiLM gain lets the shortcut dominate, nullifying the dilated benefit. |
| 7️⃣ | **Resource‑Token Scheduler replacing MoE** – shallow MLP (≤2 layers, 32 units) on concatenated raw budget + time produces a smoothed token for FiLM; routing loss removed. | Eliminates stochastic routing and its auxiliary budget, freeing ~5 % loss capacity for the primary objective; deterministic token improves gradient magnitude. | – Scheduler must be trained jointly; a too‑deep MLP can re‑introduce over‑parameterisation. |

*All patterns assume strict `(B, T, D)` tensor shapes and a single well‑behaved resource token.*

---

## 3. Failure Patterns

| # | Common Root Cause | Typical Symptom |
|---|-------------------|-----------------|
| 1️⃣ | **Uncapped or mis‑scaled auxiliary weighting** (including load‑balancing loss) | Primary loss drowned; training collapses or plateaus. |
| 2️⃣ | **Shape / dimension mismatches after token manipulation** (insertion, pooling, truncation) | Attention‑mask/runtime errors; silent gradient loss. |
| 3️⃣ | **Purely additive resource injection** (no scaling) | Feature distribution shift, loss of Lipschitz continuity → instability. |
| 4️⃣ | **Stacked FiLM layers without caps or proper init** (hierarchical FiLM) | Ill‑conditioned Jacobian, vanishing/exploding gradients, large score drop. |
| 5️⃣ | **Naïve cross‑modal pooling or concatenation before FiLM** | Modality‑specific cues erased; model collapses to homogeneous representation. |
| 6️⃣ | **Missing registration of new losses with EMA‑GradNorm balancer** (or passing unsupported constructor args) | Unregistered loss grows unchecked or operator mis‑shapes, causing hidden regressions. |

*The dominant theme*: budget‑related interference or shape‑discipline violations.

---

## 4. Operator Insights (Universal Framework Rules)

1. **Shape‑Alignment Rule** – Every sequence‑processing operator must receive and emit tensors of canonical shape `(B, T, D)`. Insert explicit `reshape`/`unsqueeze` layers before the operator; assert shape at runtime.

2. **Detachable‑Auxiliary & Budget Rule** – Compute all non‑primary losses on *detached* tensors and feed them through an EMA‑based gradient‑norm balancer that caps their total contribution to ≤ 15 % of the overall gradient norm. Apply the same rule to routing losses (≥ 10 % when MoE is used).

3. **FiLM Scaling, Init & Temperature Rule** – FiLM gains are split into a *scale* (bounded by `tanh`/`sigmoid`, initialised in the linear region ≈ 0.1) and an optional *shift* near zero. Optional per‑step temperature is produced by a ≤2‑layer MLP on normalized time, bounded to (0, 1), and multiplied into the scale before modulation.

4. **Residual‑Before‑Modulation Rule** – When pairing FiLM with a long‑range core (dilated attention, causal convolution), apply FiLM **before** the residual addition to keep both gradient paths alive.

5. **Selector & Mask Sync Rule** – Any sequence‑length change (token insert, pool, truncate) must simultaneously (a) update selector offsets, (b) rebuild loss‑mask, and (c) recompute attention mask via a single utility hook.

6. **Resource‑Conditioned Routing Rule** – In any MoE/expert block, route logits are concatenated with the same FiLM resource token; the routing loss obeys the universal auxiliary‑budget cap.

7. **Hybrid Positional Encoding Rule** – Positional codes may mix sinusoidal, learned, and stochastic components, but the final vector must be projected to hidden size and bounded (e.g., `tanh`) before addition.

8. **Operator Construction Hygiene Rule** – Pass only arguments explicitly supported by an operator’s constructor; resolve symbolic expressions to concrete integers before graph compilation. Include a unit‑test asserting the operator’s output shape matches `(B, T, D)`.

---

## 5. Relevant Literature

| Ref | Core Idea | Relevance |
|-----|-----------|-----------|
| Chen *et al.*, “Decision Transformer”, 2021 | Treat RL as conditional sequence modelling; return‑conditioning. | Provides the high‑level framing for transformer‑based policies and resource tokens. |
| Pérez *et al.*, “Feature‑wise Linear Modulation (FiLM)”, 2018 | Multiplicative/additive per‑feature conditioning. | The theoretical basis for resource‑aware FiLM and temperature annealing. |
| Hassani & Shi, “Dilated Neighborhood Attention Transformer”, 2022 | Sparse dilated attention expands receptive field linearly. | Motivates residual‑before‑modulation for long‑range cores. |
| Zhou *et al.*, “Adaptive Budget Pacing via Deep RL”, 2025 | Lagrangian dual methods for soft resource constraints; EMA‑GradNorm budgeting. | Inspires capped, detached auxiliary losses. |
| Akiti *et al.*, “Expert‑Choice Mixture‑of‑Experts for Efficient Transformers”, 2026 | Sparse MoE with per‑token routing and low‑latency expert selection. | Guides resource‑conditioned routing and the need for sufficient routing‑loss budget. |

---

## 6. Trend Assessment

Across five generations the evolution moved **from naïve FiLM → MoE → hierarchical FiLM → deterministic scheduler → temperature‑annealed FiLM**. The key quantitative shifts:

* **MoE introduction (Gen 2)** caused a modest drop (‑4.7) because the routing loss was under‑budgeted, leading to uniform expert usage and gradient dilution.
* **Hierarchical FiLM stacking (Gen 3)** produced a catastrophic loss (‑165) as competing affine transforms created an ill‑conditioned Jacobian; the auxiliary loss was neither detached nor capped.
* **Scheduler substitution (Gen 4)** reclaimed the lost budget, eliminated stochastic routing, and lifted the score (+5.3).
* **Temperature annealing (Gen 5)** added a monotonic decay of FiLM gain, aligning aggressiveness with remaining resources and yielding the current best score (+5.5).

The pattern shows **budget allocation and gradient‑norm control** as the dominant determinants of success. When auxiliary budget is either absent (Gen 2) or mis‑scaled (Gen 3), performance degrades sharply. Conversely, deterministic token flows and bounded modulation consistently improve.

---

## 7. Exploration Balance

| Theme | Description | Attempts | Share | Avg Score | Best Score |
|-------|-------------|----------|-------|-----------|------------|
| resource_conditioned_film_modulation | FiLM‑style modulation of transformer hidden states using resource or timestep signals (variants: MoE experts, token schedulers, temperature scaling) | 5 | 100 % | 661.2 | 699.9 |
| **Total** | | **5** | **100 %** | | |

**Imbalance Warning**: Concentration Score = 1.000 (SEVERE). All non‑seed generations belong to a single theme, indicating an **exploitation trap**.

---

## 8. Promising Directions

*All proposals respect the universal operator insights above and avoid re‑using the over‑explored FiLM‑modulation theme.*

### Exploration‑oriented (majority)

1. **Cross‑modal attention with modality‑specific masks** – implement multi‑head attention where queries, keys, and values are masked per modality instead of pooled; keep token shapes `(B,T,D)` and detach any added losses.
2. **Learned resource‑token scheduler with planning horizon** – augment the scheduler MLP with a tiny recurrent cell that predicts future resource trajectories, still feeding FiLM but now providing a *planned* token sequence.
3. **Meta‑learning of auxiliary‑budget allocations** – use a higher‑order optimizer to adapt per‑auxiliary caps (subject to the ≤ 15 % global rule) during training, enabling dynamic re‑balancing.
4. **Sparse external memory for long‑range resource context** – integrate a differentiable key‑value store accessed via read/write heads; enforce shape‑alignment on memory slots and cap the memory‑loss using the same EMA‑budget mechanism.
5. **Dynamic expert routing with learnable load‑balancing schedule** – replace static routing loss with a schedule that ramps from 5 % to ≥12 % of total loss, ensuring early exploration of routing space while preserving gradient budget.

### Exploitation‑oriented (minority)

1. **Higher‑resolution resource token (sub‑step granularity)** – split the episode into finer time slices, embed sub‑step budget, and test its effect on FiLM without altering other components.
2. **Gradient‑norm‑capped multi‑task auxiliary suite** – add a small set of auxiliary critics (safety, diversity) each individually capped at ≤ 5 % and jointly limited to ≤ 15 % of total gradient norm.

**Priority Allocation**: Allocate **70 %** of the remaining budget to the five exploration‑oriented items (focus on cross‑modal attention and learned scheduler) and **30 %** to the two exploitation‑oriented items (high‑resolution token & multi‑task auxiliaries). This split deliberately shifts effort away from the saturated FiLM theme while preserving a modest exploitation lane for fine‑grained tuning.
04.4

Wiki knowledge base visualization

The resulting Wiki page graph connects effective patterns, failure patterns, operator insights, and literature analyses through semantic links. Selecting a page reveals its compiled content and neighboring pages.
